IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.slideteam.net/media/catalog/product/cache/1280x720/c/o/comparative_analysis_of_qualitative_research_methods_slide01.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A comparative analysis table outlines four qualitative research methods—Focus Group Interview, Verbal Protocol, Cognitive Mapping, and Critical Incident Technique—highlighting their research scope and objectives, data collection strategies, data analysis techniques, and verification of results.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.slideteam.net/media/catalog/product/cache/1280x720/s/o/software_comparative_analysis_for_qualitative_research_process_slide01.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A table compares various qualitative research software tools based on features like annotations, data visualization, media analytics, coding, sentiment analysis, and qualitative comparative analysis, with ratings and checkmarks indicating each software's functionalities.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://imgv2-2-f.scribdassets.com/img/document/751734935/original/3e1525be11/1724866074?v=1
DESCRIPTION: The document discusses qualitative analysis in comparative authoritarianism research, focusing on research methods such as face-to-face interviews, case-intimacy, and case studies, with emphasis on the conceptualization of data collection processes and the importance of in-depth, qualitative data to understand political dynamics.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Utilizes in-depth methods like interviews and observations to understand the nuances of authoritarian regimes, offering rich insights into political dynamics and behaviors.
Focuses on specific authoritarian regimes, allowing for detailed exploration of unique contexts and politico-economic conditions influencing governance and repression strategies.
Employs a systematic comparison across various authoritarian regimes, facilitating the identification of patterns and divergences in their use of digital technologies and repression.
